security_trigger_reason: code-bearing implementation changes protected support operator routes, support incident storage/replay, owner-routed support actions, and auth/canonical evidence validation

evidence: apps/core/src/platform/operator-api.ts:679-819 requires operator admission on support routes before service invocation; POST/PATCH support bodies are bounded and writes pass operator provenance into the support service.

packages/contracts/src/operator-auth.ts:190-211 and 257-273 classify support writes as SUPPORT_OPERATION and grant that route class only to support_operator while reads remain protected introspection.

apps/core/src/support/support-evidence.ts:199-231 and 443-653 redact support refs/actions/notes before hashing and persistence, add operator auth evidence refs, claim update requests before routing owner actions, and avoid rerouting on replay.

packages/db/src/support.ts:263-328, 633-756, and 814-997 enforce support-owned write surfaces, parameterized support table writes, request-id conflict/dedupe handling, applied/rejected replay state, closure-readiness blocking, and terminal reopen evidence evaluation.

apps/core/src/support/support-canonical-refs.ts:197-317 resolves report, release, and operator-auth refs through owner readers and marks missing, unavailable, stale, or degraded evidence without direct foreign writes.

.dossier/verification/F-0028/implementation-78652ba50b17.json is the current verification artifact and records passing implementation verification including support-specific tests and container smoke evidence.

notes: Security audit r10 at 78652ba50b17: no confirmed exploitable findings. Reviewed protected support route admission and RBAC, bounded pre-auth support body handling, support incident persistence and replay, owner-routed action evidence handling, canonical reporting/release/auth evidence validation, terminal closure preservation, and incident data exposure.
